vertical_remap.accuracy: O(dp^3) interior; degrades to O(dp) within two cells of the column top/bottom and at active CW84 limiter constraints
vertical_remap.reference: Lin (2004), MWR 132(10):2293-2307, eqs. (16)-(18); reconstruction per Colella & Woodward (1984), JCP 54(1):174-201, eqs. (1.6)-(1.10) with the (1.7)-(1.10) monotonicity limiter

inputs.q: cell-averaged scalar on the old vertical layers (column shape [Nk])
inputs.dp_old: old layer thicknesses (column shape [Nk]); units cancel in q_new
inputs.dp_new: new layer thicknesses (column shape [Nk]); sum_k dp_new[k] should equal sum_k dp_old[k] for column-mass invariance

edge_value_stencil.comment: PPM 4th-order interior interpolation of cell-averaged q to layer interfaces, same coefficients as ppm_reconstruction.json (CW84 eq. 1.6). Re-stated here so vertical_remap is readable standalone; the runtime evaluator may share the materialization with ppm_reconstruction once a `uses_rule` reference syntax lands.
edge_value_stencil.produces: q_{k+1/2} (right interface of layer k, left interface of layer k+1)

boundary_handling.comment: Column top/bottom degrade because the 4-cell support of the 4th-order CW84 edge interpolation runs out. Mirrors src/operators/vertical_remap.jl _remap_column!.

k_eq_1_or_Nk.order: O(1) — constant reconstruction on the boundary layer

k_eq_2_or_Nk_minus_1.order: O(dp^2) — 2nd-order centered average to interior interfaces, then CW84 limiter

interior.rule: use the 4-point CW84 stencil above on each interface, then CW84 limiter

limiter.comment: Colella & Woodward (1984) §4 monotonicity limiter for the parabolic profile. Implemented as a closed-form ExpressionNode AST so the rule is evaluator-agnostic; matches src/operators/reconstruction.jl _ppm_limit_cw84_sym (the symbolic-tracing-safe form using ifelse) bit-for-bit at the AST level.
limiter.name: colella_woodward_1984

is_extremum.comment: True when q[k] is a local extremum within the column — flatten the parabola to a constant.

overshoot_left.comment: Right edge sits past the parabola's interior extremum; pull the LEFT edge inward.

overshoot_right.comment: Left edge sits past the parabola's interior extremum; pull the RIGHT edge inward.

parabola.comment: Within each old layer k, the limited (q_left[k], q_right[k], q[k]) triple defines the CW84 sub-grid parabola (eqs. 1.5, 1.7, 1.10). Local coordinate xi in [0,1] runs from the layer's lower interface (xi=0) to its upper interface (xi=1).
parabola.a_L: q_left[k]   (q at lower interface of layer k after CW84 limiting)
parabola.a_R: q_right[k]  (q at upper interface of layer k after CW84 limiting)

indefinite_integral.comment: Closed-form antiderivative of the parabola in local coordinate xi, used by the conservative remap sweep (Lin 2004 eq. 16). Mass over a sub-interval [xi_lo, xi_hi] within old layer k is (F(xi_hi) - F(xi_lo)) * dp_old[k].

remap_sweep.comment: Conservative remap sweep is *not* a fixed stencil over neighbors — the set of old layers that overlap a given new layer depends on the cumulative-pressure intersection of dp_old with dp_new and varies per-column. The schema gap is the conservative-remap rule type tracked in docs/rule-catalog.md (`vertical_remap_lin_2004` row) and the time-varying per-cell metric binding tracked as decision #3 in SELECTOR_KINDS.md. The algorithm is captured here in symbolic prose so the rule is reproducible from this file alone; runtime materialization currently lives in src/operators/vertical_remap.jl::_remap_column! pending the schema extension.

interfaces.comment: Cumulative pressure at layer interfaces, k = 0..Nk; reference p_old[0] = p_new[0] = 0 (only differences matter under conservation).

per_new_layer.comment: For each new layer kn in 1..Nk, integrate the (limited) PPM parabolas of every old layer ko whose interface span [p_old[ko-1], p_old[ko]] intersects [p_new[kn-1], p_new[kn]], summing mass and dividing by dp_new[kn].
per_new_layer.integral: integral[kn] = sum_{ko: overlap} (F(xi(min(p_new[kn], p_old[ko]), ko)) - F(xi(max(p_new[kn-1], p_old[ko-1]), ko))) * dp_old[ko]
per_new_layer.output: q_new[kn] = integral[kn] / dp_new[kn]   (degenerate dp_new[kn] -> 0 falls back to q_new[kn] = q[ko] for the bracketing old layer; matches the dp < 1e-30 short-circuit in the reference implementation)

conservation_invariant.comment: Column-integrated mass is preserved exactly in real arithmetic; floating-point error is bounded by O(Nk * eps * sum_k |q[k]| * dp_old[k]).
conservation_invariant.identity: sum_kn q_new[kn] * dp_new[kn] = sum_ko q[ko] * dp_old[ko]   (when sum_kn dp_new[kn] = sum_ko dp_old[ko])

schema_gaps.comment: Two ESS schema extensions are required before this rule is fully materializable from JSON alone. Until they land, the runtime path is src/operators/vertical_remap.jl. The rule file is authored declaratively (matching the weno5_advection.json precedent for nonlinear_weights) so the math, references, and AST fragments are reproducible.

conservative_remap_rule_type.tracker: docs/rule-catalog.md row `vertical_remap_lin_2004` (schema_gaps: needs conservative-remap rule type)
conservative_remap_rule_type.summary: ESS §7 stencils encode a fixed neighbor list combined by `+`/`*`/min/max. Conservative remap is a variable-arity overlap sweep whose neighbor list is data-dependent (cumulative-pressure intersection of dp_old with dp_new). The natural shape is a new rule kind that emits an `arrayop` over old layers gated by the overlap predicate, parallel in spirit to the §4 `reduction` selector for unstructured grids.

time_varying_vertical_metric_binding.summary: Layer thicknesses dp_old and dp_new vary in time and (in the Lagrangian-vertical use case) per column; the ESS MMS harness today binds only scalar per-axis spacing symbols. Until per-cell time-varying metric bindings land, walker fixtures for this rule must declare applicable=false.
